name: moon-forming-impact
description: A Mars-sized body struck the young Earth, and debris from the collision coalesced into the Moon.

name: earth-oceans-form
description: As the molten surface cooled, water vapor condensed into rain and filled the planet's first oceans.

name: late-heavy-bombardment
description: A surge of asteroids and comets battered the inner planets, scarring the young Earth and Moon.

name: first-cyanobacteria
description: Microbes that release oxygen through photosynthesis emerged, slowly transforming the atmosphere.

name: oldest-stromatolites
description: Layered mats built by microbial colonies created some of the oldest visible records of life on Earth.

name: first-eukaryotic-cells
description: Cells with a nucleus arose, likely when one microbe engulfed another, paving the way for complex life.

name: first-multicellular-life
description: Cells began cooperating in groups, marking an early step from single cells toward complex organisms.

name: ediacaran-biota
description: Soft-bodied creatures formed Earth's earliest complex animal communities on the ancient seafloor.

name: cambrian-explosion
description: Most major animal groups appeared in a relatively short burst, filling the seas with diverse life.

name: first-trilobites
description: These armored sea creatures became some of the most successful and widespread early animals.

name: first-vertebrates
description: The earliest animals with backbones appeared in the oceans, ancestors of all later vertebrate life.

name: first-coral-reefs
description: Reef-building organisms began constructing vast underwater structures teeming with marine life.

name: plants-colonize-land
description: Simple plants spread from water onto the continents, greening the once-barren land surfaces.

name: ordovician-mass-extinction
description: Global cooling wiped out much marine life in one of the largest extinctions in Earth's history.

name: first-jawed-fish
description: Fish evolved hinged jaws, transforming them into active predators and reshaping ocean food chains.

name: arthropods-colonize-land
description: Early arthropods such as millipedes crawled ashore, becoming the first animals to live on land.

name: age-of-fishes
description: Fish diversified across the world's seas, dominating the oceans during the Devonian Period.

name: first-forests
description: Tree-like plants formed Earth's earliest forests, drawing down carbon and changing the climate.

name: fish-walk-onto-land
description: Lobe-finned fish with limb-like fins began moving onto land, foreshadowing four-legged animals.

name: late-devonian-extinction
description: A drawn-out series of die-offs devastated marine life, especially reef-building organisms.

name: first-amniote-egg
description: Animals evolved tough-shelled eggs, freeing them from water and letting them spread across dry land.

name: giant-insects-of-carboniferous
description: High oxygen levels let dragonflies grow to hawk-sized wingspans in vast Carboniferous swamps.

name: pangaea-forms
description: Drifting landmasses merged into one giant continent, Pangaea, surrounded by a single global ocean.

name: great-dying-extinction
description: Earth's worst extinction killed most marine and land species, nearly ending complex life entirely.

name: first-dinosaurs-appear
description: Small, fast reptiles emerged that would diversify into the dinosaurs ruling Earth for ages.

name: first-mammals-evolve
description: Tiny, shrew-like creatures became the first mammals, living in the shadow of the early dinosaurs.

name: pangaea-breaks-apart
description: The supercontinent began splitting, slowly opening oceans and drifting toward today's continents.

name: age-of-dinosaurs
description: Dinosaurs dominated land ecosystems worldwide during the Jurassic, growing to enormous sizes.

name: first-flowering-plants
description: Flowers evolved and spread rapidly, transforming landscapes and feeding pollinating insects.

name: first-bees
description: Bees evolved alongside flowers, forming a partnership that shaped plant life across the planet.

name: tyrannosaurus-roams
description: The huge predator Tyrannosaurus rex hunted across North America in the final age of the dinosaurs.

name: first-grasses
description: Grasses spread across the world after the dinosaurs, eventually forming vast plains and savannas.

name: first-primates
description: Early tree-dwelling mammals with grasping hands appeared, ancestors of monkeys, apes, and humans.

name: whales-return-to-sea
description: Land mammals gradually evolved into whales, abandoning the shore to live entirely in the oceans.

name: himalayas-rise
description: India collided with Asia, crumpling the crust upward to build the towering Himalayan mountains.

name: first-apes
description: Tailless primates known as apes appeared in Africa, the lineage leading toward gorillas and humans.

name: human-chimp-split
description: The lineages leading to humans and chimpanzees split from a common ancestor in Africa.

name: first-upright-walkers
description: Early human relatives in Africa began walking on two legs, freeing their hands for other tasks.

name: lucy-australopithecus-lived
description: Lucy, a small upright Australopithecus, lived in Africa and became a famous human ancestor fossil.

name: woolly-mammoths-appear
description: Shaggy, cold-adapted mammoths spread across the frozen steppes of Ice Age Europe and Asia.

name: saber-toothed-cats
description: Powerful cats with long curved fangs preyed on large Ice Age mammals across the Americas.

name: ice-age-megafauna-extinction
description: Mammoths, giant sloths, and other huge mammals vanished as the last Ice Age ended worldwide.

name: dog-becomes-companion
description: Wolves drawn to human camps gradually became dogs, the first animals tamed by people.

name: first-crops-cultivated
description: Peoples of the Fertile Crescent began planting and harvesting wild grains, dawning farming.

name: cattle-domesticated
description: Wild aurochs were tamed into cattle, giving early farmers meat, milk, and muscle power.

name: cats-domesticated
description: Wild cats moved into early farming villages to hunt rodents, beginning a long bond with humans.

name: silkworm-domesticated
description: Chinese farmers raised silkworms on mulberry leaves to harvest threads for weaving silk.

name: giant-ground-sloths-roam
description: Elephant-sized ground sloths lumbered across the Americas during the Ice Age before dying out.

name: honeybee-keeping-begins
description: Ancient Egyptians kept honeybees in clay hives, harvesting honey and wax for food and trade.

name: aristotle-studies-animals
description: The Greek philosopher Aristotle described and classified hundreds of species, founding biology.

name: shen-kuo-explains-fossils
description: Chinese scholar Shen Kuo recognized seashell fossils inland as evidence of ancient seas.

name: steno-fossils-rock-layers
description: Nicolas Steno argued that rock layers form in order over time, founding the study of geology.

name: leeuwenhoek-microbes
description: Antonie van Leeuwenhoek used his microscope to observe tiny living organisms for the first time.

name: linnaeus-systema-naturae
description: Carl Linnaeus introduced a system of two-part Latin names to classify all living things.

name: hutton-deep-time
description: James Hutton argued Earth was shaped by slow processes over an unimaginably vast span of time.

name: cuvier-proves-extinction
description: Georges Cuvier showed that fossil animals like mastodons were species that no longer existed.

name: smith-geological-map
description: William Smith mapped England's rock strata using fossils, founding modern geological mapping.

name: anning-finds-ichthyosaur
description: Fossil hunter Mary Anning uncovered ancient marine reptiles on England's Dorset coast.

name: lyell-principles-geology
description: Charles Lyell's book argued that gradual forces still at work had shaped Earth over ages.

name: dinosaur-word-coined
description: Richard Owen named the great extinct reptiles dinosaurs, meaning terrible lizards.

name: ice-age-theory-proposed
description: Louis Agassiz proposed that vast ice sheets once covered much of Europe and North America.

name: neanderthal-fossil-found
description: Bones found in Germany's Neander Valley revealed a distinct extinct human cousin.

name: archaeopteryx-discovered
description: A feathered fossil linking dinosaurs and birds was discovered in Germany, supporting evolution.

name: pasteur-disproves-spontaneous
description: Louis Pasteur showed that microbes come from other microbes, not from lifeless matter.

name: last-aurochs-dies
description: The last aurochs, the wild ancestor of cattle, died in Poland, marking the species' extinction.

name: dodo-driven-extinct
description: The flightless dodo of Mauritius vanished within decades of sailors reaching the island.

name: wallace-line-described
description: Alfred Russel Wallace charted a sharp boundary dividing the animals of Asia and Australia.

name: last-quagga-dies
description: The quagga, a half-striped relative of the zebra, became extinct in an Amsterdam zoo.

name: passenger-pigeon-extinct
description: Once numbering billions, the passenger pigeon went extinct when the last bird died in a zoo.

name: wegener-continental-drift
description: Alfred Wegener proposed that continents slowly move across the globe over millions of years.

name: java-man-discovered
description: Eugene Dubois found fossils in Indonesia of Homo erectus, an early upright human ancestor.

name: tutankhamun-fossil-coelacanth
description: A coelacanth, a fish thought extinct for millions of years, was caught off South Africa.

name: last-thylacine-dies
description: The thylacine, a striped marsupial predator, went extinct when the last one died in a zoo.

name: miller-urey-experiment
description: Scientists sparked simple chemicals into amino acids, hinting how life's building blocks arose.

name: olduvai-tools-found
description: The Leakeys uncovered early human fossils and stone tools at Olduvai Gorge in Tanzania.

name: seafloor-spreading-confirmed
description: Magnetic stripes on the ocean floor proved new crust forms there, confirming plate tectonics.

name: asteroid-killed-dinosaurs
description: Walter and Luis Alvarez proposed an asteroid impact wiped out the dinosaurs 66 million years ago.

name: chicxulub-crater-confirmed
description: A vast buried crater in Mexico was identified as the asteroid impact that doomed the dinosaurs.

name: ardi-fossil-described
description: Scientists unveiled Ardi, a 4.4-million-year-old skeleton revealing how early humans walked.

name: denisovans-identified
description: DNA from a finger bone in Siberia revealed Denisovans, a previously unknown human cousin.

name: northern-white-rhino-decline
description: Sudan, the last male northern white rhino, died, leaving the subspecies functionally extinct.
